mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held near historic lows for multiple consecutive months keeps the equity risk premium compressed relative to bond yields, producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as investors continue rotating into equities for yield.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns during low-rate periods are not statistically higher than the full-sample mean (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below a critical low threshold signaling a tight labor market, wage-cost pressure raises fears of Fed tightening, causing S&P 500 returns to weaken over the subsequent trading week as markets price higher future policy rates.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below 4.5% while FEDFUNDS is rising month-over-month
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tightening labor market
failure_condition: In periods when UNRATE < 4.5% and FEDFUNDS rising, 5-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The joint behavior of accelerating CPI inflation alongside a rising federal funds rate signals a deteriorating real-rate environment; when both series rise together, the combined tightening effect on real yields depresses equity valuations more than either series alone would predict over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change > 2.5% AND FEDFUNDS rising for 3 consecutive months
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with rising inflation
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns following joint triggers are not significantly lower than unconditional baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC of the interaction term is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts during a late-cycle economic expansion signal robust consumer confidence and forward construction activity, feeding into broader demand expectations that lift equity prices over the following quarter; this effect is specific to expansions and reverses in early-cycle or recessionary regimes.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with positive GDP growth
failure_condition: During periods of positive year-over-year GDPC1 growth, HOUST increases are not associated with statistically significant positive 60-day forward SPX returns (p > 0.05), or the sign of the effect flips in more than one out of three expansion regimes tested.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and corporate earnings capacity, driving a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns over the following month as analysts revise earnings estimates upward.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO 3-month change and forward 20d returns is below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or positive INDPRO changes precede negative SPX returns in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration in retail sales growth below a defined threshold signals weakening consumer demand, prompting a short-term negative reaction in S&P 500 returns over the following trading week as markets price reduced corporate revenue expectations.
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns following negative RSAFS growth are not significantly lower than baseline (p > 0.05), or fewer than 50% of triggered instances show negative returns.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong payroll growth and a falling unemployment rate jointly indicate an overheating labor market approaching capacity constraints; this combination raises inflation and rate-hike expectations, producing a larger negative equity reaction over the following quarter than either indicator alone.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average change > 200k AND UNRATE declines > 0.2pp over same period
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns after joint triggers are not significantly negative relative to baseline (p > 0.05), or the interaction Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of below-trend real GDP growth, equity markets exhibit heightened sensitivity to incoming demand data, such that positive quarterly GDP surprises produce outsized positive S&P 500 forward returns compared to their effect during robust growth regimes, reflecting reduced growth-risk premia.
failure_condition: In quarters with sub-2% annualized GDPC1 growth, subsequent 60-day forward SPX returns are not significantly higher than in above-trend growth quarters (p > 0.05), or the effect direction reverses in more than one of three tested slowdown episodes.
